section_title: The 25 Great Opera Houses of the World
intro: From the gilded horseshoes of 18th-century Italy to the parametric curves of 21st-century Beijing, the world's leading opera houses form a global circuit linking Europe's historic capitals to the Americas, Asia, and Australia. This list weighs reputation, architectural significance, programming ambition, capacity, and the artists who currently shape each stage in the 2025/26 and 2026/27 seasons.

houses.name: Metropolitan Opera
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Peter Gelb (General Manager); Yannick Nézet-Séguin (Music Director)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Largest repertory opera house in the world; flagship of American opera at Lincoln Center since 1966.

houses.name: Teatro alla Scala
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Fortunato Ortombina (Sovrintendente, from Aug 2025); Riccardo Chailly (Music Director through 2026)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: The temple of Italian opera; site of premieres by Verdi, Puccini and Bellini, with the storied Dec 7 Sant'Ambrogio gala opening.

houses.name: Royal Opera House
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Jakub Hrůša (Music Director, from 2025/26); Alex Beard (Chief Executive)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Covent Garden home of The Royal Opera and The Royal Ballet; UK's flagship lyric stage.

houses.name: Wiener Staatsoper
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Bogdan Roščić (Direktor)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Vienna's Ringstrasse jewel; nightly repertory of the Vienna Philharmonic in the pit and the world's deepest opera schedule.

houses.name: Bayerische Staatsoper (Nationaltheater München)
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Serge Dorny (Staatsintendant, contract through 2031); Vladimir Jurowski (GMD through 2028)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Germany's largest opera house and historic Wagner stronghold; host of the Munich Opera Festival.

houses.name: Opéra national de Paris (Palais Garnier & Opéra Bastille)
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Alexander Neef (Director General, second mandate through 2031/32)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Twin-house institution: Garnier's gilded Belle Époque hall and the modern 2,723-seat Bastille define French opera.

houses.name: Teatro La Fenice
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Nicola Colabianchi (Sovrintendente, from 2024)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Phoenix theatre rebuilt three times after fire; site of premieres of Rigoletto, La traviata and Tosca's Venetian sister works.

houses.name: Teatro di San Carlo
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Carlo Fuortes (Sovrintendente); Dan Ettinger (Music Director)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Oldest continuously active opera house in Europe; Bourbon-era horseshoe that shaped the bel canto era.

houses.name: Teatro Real
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Ignacio García-Belenguer (Director General); Gustavo Gimeno (Music Director)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Spain's premier opera house; reopened 1997 with one of Europe's most advanced stages, on Plaza de Oriente by the Royal Palace.

houses.name: Gran Teatre del Liceu
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Valentí Oviedo (Director General); Jonathan Nott (Music Director from 2026/27)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Catalonia's grand opera house on La Rambla; rebuilt after the 1994 fire as one of the largest theatres in Europe.

houses.name: Bolshoi Theatre
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Valery Gergiev (General Director, from Dec 2023)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Russia's national opera and ballet stage marking its 250th jubilee in 2026; defining home of Tchaikovsky and the Russian repertoire.

houses.name: Mariinsky Theatre
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Valery Gergiev (Artistic & General Director since 1988)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Imperial-era horseshoe and birthplace of Russian opera and ballet; expanded with the 1,830-seat Mariinsky II in 2013.

houses.name: Deutsche Oper Berlin
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Aviel Cahn (Intendant from 2025/26); Sir Donald Runnicles (GMD)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: West Berlin's modernist opera house; the city's largest, with a strong Wagner and 20th-century repertoire.

houses.name: Staatsoper Unter den Linden
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Elisabeth Sobotka (Intendant); Christian Thielemann (GMD from 2024/25)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Frederick the Great's Lindenoper; restored 2017 with the historic Staatskapelle Berlin in the pit.

houses.name: Semperoper
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Nora Schmid (Intendantin); Daniele Gatti (Chefdirigent of Sächsische Staatskapelle, from 2024)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Gottfried Semper's Renaissance Revival masterwork; site of nine Richard Strauss premieres including Salome and Elektra.

houses.name: Staatsoper Hamburg
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Tobias Kratzer (Intendant from 2025/26); Omer Meir Wellber (GMD from 2025/26)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Germany's oldest civic opera house, dating to 1678; a major Northern stage with a strong contemporary line.

houses.name: Dutch National Opera (Nationale Opera)
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Sophie de Lint (Director Opera); Lorenzo Viotti (Chief Conductor)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Adventurous Stopera house at the Amstel; one of Europe's most consistent commissioners of new opera.

houses.name: La Monnaie / De Munt
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Christina Scheppelmann (General & Artistic Director from July 2025); Alain Altinoglu (Music Director)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Belgium's national opera; cradle of the 1830 revolution and a leading European house for theatrically daring productions.

houses.name: Royal Swedish Opera (Kungliga Operan)
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Fredrik Lindgren (General Manager since July 2022); Alan Gilbert (Music Director)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Sweden's national opera since 1773; Gustav III's Enlightenment legacy in a neo-classical Stockholm landmark.

houses.name: Lyric Opera of Chicago
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: John Mangum (General Director, President & CEO); Enrique Mazzola (Music Director through 2031)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: North America's second-largest opera auditorium; a Wacker Drive Art Deco landmark and Midwest opera flagship.

houses.name: San Francisco Opera (War Memorial Opera House)
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Matthew Shilvock (General Director); Eun Sun Kim (Music Director, contract through 2030/31)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Beaux-Arts civic temple where the UN Charter was signed in 1945; the West Coast's leading opera company.

houses.name: Sydney Opera House (Joan Sutherland Theatre)
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Fiona Allan (CEO Opera Australia); Jaime Martín (Music Director Opera Australia)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: UNESCO-listed Utzon icon on Bennelong Point; home of Opera Australia and the most photographed performing arts venue on earth.

houses.name: Teatro Colón
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Jorge Telerman (Director General)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Latin America's grandest opera house; rated by acoustician Leo Beranek as having the best acoustics for opera in the world.

houses.name: National Centre for the Performing Arts (NCPA) — Opera House
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Wang Ning (President of NCPA); Lü Jia (Music Director, NCPA Orchestra)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Paul Andreu's titanium-and-glass 'Giant Egg' beside Tiananmen Square; Asia's largest theatre complex and China's flagship opera stage.

houses.name: Bayreuth Festspielhaus (Bayreuther Festspiele)
houses.current_music_director_or_general_manage: Katharina Wagner (Festival Director)
houses.one_line_reputational_note: Wagner's purpose-built festival theatre with covered orchestra pit; the world's most singular opera shrine, marking 150 years in 2026.
